summary: Frontend-only validation of the new shared BaseTicket primitive and 6 service-specific ticket components rendered through OrderDetailModal. Logged in as customer@test.com on /orders and opened tickets across all available service types (Event, Travel, Hotel, Car Rental). Cinema/Restaurant/Laundry orders are not present in this account, so they were validated via code/structure inspection. All four live tickets render with the Event-style two-panel paradigm (brand band + perforation + LEFT poster/badges/meta-grid/extras + RIGHT dark info+rules panel). Modal scaffolding (operator strip, customer block, payment summary, money trail) all intact. No real frontend console errors triggered by the ticket components.

playwright_results.cinema_ticket: SKIPPED - no cinema order in customer@test.com account. Code inspection: /app/frontend/src/components/tickets/CinemaTicket.jsx exists, exports testId='cinema-ticket', uses BaseTicket with accent #0891b2, includes ticket-film-genres / ticket-film-credits / ticket-film-synopsis / ticket-film-trailer-link sub-testids. OrderDetailModal line 379-382 conditionally renders <CinemaTicket/> for service_type='cinema'.
playwright_results.restaurant_ticket: SKIPPED - no restaurant order. Code inspection: RestaurantTicket.jsx exists, testId='restaurant-ticket', BaseTicket accent #c2410c, restaurant-ticket-items sub-testid for pre-ordered items. OrderDetailModal lines 399-402 wire it up.
playwright_results.laundry_ticket: SKIPPED - no laundry order. Code inspection: LaundryTicket.jsx exists, testId='laundry-ticket', BaseTicket accent #7e22ce, laundry-ticket-items + laundry-ticket-price-summary sub-testids. OrderDetailModal lines 389-392 wire it up.

code_structure_check.tickets_dir: 7 files present (BaseTicket, EventTicket, TravelTicket, CinemaTicket, HotelTicket, RestaurantTicket, CarRentalTicket, LaundryTicket) -- PASS
code_structure_check.old_inline_testids_removed: grep for order-vehicle-info | order-screening-info | order-laundry-info | order-luggage-manifest returned 0 matches in OrderDetailModal.jsx -- PASS
code_structure_check.new_ticket_testids_defined: All 7 testIds set as BaseTicket testId prop in respective wrappers -- PASS

console_errors: No new errors introduced by ticket components. Pre-existing noise only: 'Error fetching permissions' (network/auth context) and ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED for external image hosts (operator logos etc.) — neither related to the ticket refactor.
critical_code_review_comments: Excellent refactor: ~480 lines (≈43%) eliminated from OrderDetailModal by extracting per-service tickets behind a shared BaseTicket primitive — strong DRY win.
BaseTicket is well-parameterised (accentColor, posterAspect, badges, metaItems, extraSections, rightPanelTitle/Description, rules) which makes future service additions trivial.

Minor: BaseTicket.jsx uses inline style={{ borderColor: `${ACCENT}40` }} pattern in 2 child components — works fine but consider extracting a tiny helper to avoid the hex-suffix opacity convention being copy-pasted across files.
No accessibility regressions observed in modal close/Esc dismissal flow.
Travel ticket correctly handles the 'Extra Luggage Manifest (2)' edge case with both bag items rendering individually testable.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested types (Event, Travel, Hotel, Car Rental); 3 types not tested due to absent seed orders but verified by code inspection

context_for_next_testing_agent: If a future iteration adds seed cinema/restaurant/laundry orders for customer@test.com, just open each via :has-text(service_name) selector and assert data-testid in [cinema-ticket, restaurant-ticket, laundry-ticket]. The conditional renderers in OrderDetailModal (lines 374-407) are confirmed wired correctly — only missing the orders to drive them. Modal close uses Esc key reliably.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. Refactor is complete, visually polished, and behaviour-equivalent across the four service types we could observe live.
